translation_or_gloss: tin allocated to a man from Caphtor/Kaptara
source_provenance: Dossin tin text; cited Wikipedia Caphtor + Strange 1980; Malamat 1971 IEJ 21:31 (tin inventory)
editorial_notes: Transmission language AKKADIAN — semantic anchor only (commodity=tin), NO phonetic value for Linear A. Interpreter of the chief merchant of the Caphtorians attested at Ugarit.

translation_or_gloss: a Caphtorite (inlaid metal) weapon, to Zimri-Lim
source_provenance: Wiener (Bronze Age Trade, open) citing Mari tablets; Wikipedia Caphtor
editorial_notes: Commodity class: ceremonial/inlaid metal weapon. Akkadian descriptor 'Kaptarite'.

scholarly_transliteration: a pair of clothing and a pair of leather shoes from Kaptara
editorial_notes: Specific text ref ARMT 342:4-12. Commodity classes: textile/clothing + leather goods.

original_text: GAL kap-ta-ri-tum / bronze vessels and pincers from Kaptaru
scholarly_transliteration: kaptaritum vessel (large jar); bronze vessels + pincers from Kaptaru
source_provenance: Wiener; Wikipedia Caphtor (GAL kaptaritum = large jug/jar)
editorial_notes: Commodity classes: metal vessels + tools (pincers). Also katapum Kaptaruu = a Caphtorite object, Zimri-Lim -> King Shariya of Razama.

original_text: targumannu (interpreter of the chief merchant of the Caphtorians at Ugarit)
scholarly_transliteration: interpreter (targumannu) of the wakil tamkari of the Kaptarites at Ugarit
translation_or_gloss: interpreter of the chief merchant of the Caphtorians resident at Ugarit
candidate_modern_identification: Minoan/Cretan merchant community + interpreter at Ugarit
findspot: Mari tin text; community at Ugarit
editorial_notes: ROLE anchor: confirms an interpreted Cretan/Kaptaran merchant presence at Ugarit (18th c). Interpreter => the languages were mutually unintelligible (Minoan vs Akkadian) - directly relevant to the identifiability argument.

original_text: annakum (tin) to the Cretans + interpreter at Ugarit
scholarly_transliteration: 1+x/3 minas tin (to the Cretans); 1/3 mina tin (to the) interpreter of the Chief tamkar (trader) of the Cretans in Ugarit
translation_or_gloss: tin allocated to the Cretans and to their chief-merchant's interpreter at Ugarit
source_provenance: Hojen Sorensen 2009 citing Heltzer 1989:12 and Cline 1994:126 D; Dossin tin text
editorial_notes: Precise: tin measured in minas; interpreter (targumannu) of the wakil tamkari of the Cretans at Ugarit. Akkadian - semantic/role anchor, no LA phonetics.

original_text: giš-tukul kap-ta-ru-ù mu-ha-šu ù i-ši-is-sú-ú kù-gi uh-hu-uz ... ta-am-li na4-za-gìn
scholarly_transliteration: gištukul kaptaruu ... kù-gi (gold) uhhuz ... tamli na4-zagin (lapis)
translation_or_gloss: a Cretan mace-weapon, its top gold-mounted and inlaid with lapis lazuli
source_provenance: Hojen Sorensen 2009 citing Dossin 1939:112, Guichard 1999:171 ('une arme cretoise'), Cline 1994:127
editorial_notes: THE ceremonial mace: giš-tukul (weapon), gold-mounted (kù-gi), lapis-inlaid (na4-za-gin). Adjective kap-ta-ru-u / Kaptarayum.

original_text: 1 gal kap-ta-ri-tum kù-GI (ARM 31,227; ARM 25,523; ARM 24,91; ARM 25,526)
scholarly_transliteration: gal kaptaritum kù-GI 2/3 ma-na — Caphtorian gold vase(s); 'two Caphtorian vases with engraved decorations, without handles'
editorial_notes: PRECISE text refs (ARM 31/25/24). gal=vase, kù-GI=gold, weight in minas. Supersedes v1 'ARM-verify'.

original_text: Anaku and Kaptara, the lands across the Upper Sea
scholarly_transliteration: Anaku u Kaptara ... aban Upper Sea
translation_or_gloss: Kaptara (with Anaku) named as lands across the Upper Sea in the Sargon 'King of Battle' legend
findspot: literary (sar tamhari / King of Battle tradition)
editorial_notes: LITERARY, not administrative; tier-D exploratory. Included only as external toponym attestation of Kaptara across the Upper Sea. Do not use as an anchor.
